pages.raw_text:
The trouble between the local
printers union and the Review
Publishing Co. assumed unique
and complicated aspect today. Its
outcome may be an application for
Injunction to prevent the interna-
tional executive board removing
the Spokane unions charter, filed
on behalf of other members not
concerned in the Review trouble.


The dispute Is expected to come
to a head at meeting called for
o'clock this afternoon at which
President Lynch, of the 1 nterna-
tional organization, will be repre-
scented by Daniel Cartano, Of this
city.


Late yesterday afternoon Presi-
dent Lynch wired an ultimatum
that the Spokane unions charter
would be revoked unless the men,
bers who quit work returned to-
day. Mr.. Cartano was delegated a
personal representative Of the
president to carry out the condi-
lions imposed.


President Lynch has telegraphed
to Butte and Anaconda and other
western towns to send all idle
printers to this city. Cartano met
the men who have left their posi-
lions on the Spokesman-Review
and Chronicle and advised them
OF obey the orders of the interna-
tional and return to work pending
arbitration of the present trouble.
None of the men, however have re-
turned to their places and none
show a disposition to do so. They
have no objection to other union
men coming in and have so mott
fed Mr. Cartano. They simply
don't want the Jobs.


The prospective situation Inn
piled by the presidents action af-
fects the balance of the union in
this city, who are not involved in
the existing trouble, to this ex-
tent: II the charter is revoked
4 they find themselves without union
standing. All other newspapers,
printing shops and trades allied in
the printing business find them
selves deprived of the union label.
This Is the view of 1t taken by
many union members, and a
strong sentiment of remonstrance
has developed.


The outcome depends on the re-
suits of this afternoons meeting.
The men who quit say they will
not return. Lynch says they
must.
